title: JWST WASP-39 b Transmission-Spectrum Evidence Ladder
question: How stable are simple nested-model feature preferences in a real published WASP-39 b transmission spectrum under bootstrap and wavelength-segment sensitivity?
gap: A transparent model-comparison bridge; not a full atmospheric retrieval or replacement for TauREx/petitRADTRANS.
dataMode: real public JWST NIRSpec G395H spectrum products
novelty: A transparent model-comparison bridge; not a full atmospheric retrieval or replacement for TauREx/petitRADTRANS.

figures.label: Spectrum overlays (data vs. no-CO vs. full model)

validationContract: synthetic injection-recovery gate (known Gaussian feature recovered)
null control (no feature injected, complex model not preferred)
weighted chi-square / AIC / BIC evidence ladder
methodology: The real, published WASP-39b transmission spectrum and the paper's own best-fit full (with CO) and no-CO nested models (Grant, Lothringer, Wakeford et al. 2023, Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7866690) are loaded directly from the deposited netCDF product. A weighted chi-square, AIC and BIC evidence ladder compares the two real published models against the real data (no re-fitting of the physical retrieval). A bootstrap (1000 resamples, seed 20260713) estimates a confidence interval on the mean residual amplitude within the CO absorption band (defined as the wavelength range where the CO-only model component exceeds 10% of its own peak). A leave-one-wavelength-segment-out check splits the native-resolution G395H bandpass into three contiguous segments and re-runs the evidence ladder on each, reporting whether the model preference is stable across the bandpass. Separately, a synthetic flat-continuum vs. flat-plus-Gaussian model pair (models.py, fitting.py) provides a required injection-recovery validation gate independent of the real published models.
assumptions: The no-CO to full-model step is treated as adding exactly 1 effective free parameter (the CO abundance/opacity term); the real published atmospheric retrieval's exact free-parameter count is not recoverable from the netCDF file alone. Documented as a defensible simplification, not a fabricated exact count.

The native-resolution wavelength grid is split into three contiguous segments as a practical proxy for 'instrument segment', since this is a single-grating (G395H) dataset rather than a multi-instrument spectrum.
The CO absorption band is operationally defined as wavelengths where the paper's own CO-only model component exceeds 10% of its own peak value, not an independently derived band edge.
limitations: A transparent model-comparison bridge over the paper's own published models; not a full atmospheric retrieval and not a replacement for TauREx/petitRADTRANS.

Only one real target (WASP-39b, single visit's reduced spectrum) is available from the 60.3 KB Zenodo product actually used; the originally-scoped larger multi-target Zenodo deposit (10.5281/zenodo.10161743, 1.74 GB) was rejected as too large for a bounded first release.
reports/report.tex could not be compiled to PDF in this environment (no local LaTeX toolchain); only structural completeness and real-number substitution were verified.
